title: pittosporum viridiflorum var. kruegeri
intro: Pittosporum viridiflorum var. kruegeri, also known as the Ivory Pear or Kruger's Pigeonwood, is a variety of the Pittosporum viridiflorum plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Pittosporaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum viridiflorum var. kruegeri include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
